page: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is an x86_64 architecture server within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, backed by 2 physical cores. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage is provided via a bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core execution. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the server achieves top-tier performance, placing in the top 10%. However, multi-core stress-ng performance drops to the middle 50% average, and the overall PassMark CPU score falls into the bottom 25% weak tier. Memory performance is mixed, with large-block read and write bandwidths performing at average levels, while small-block cached read bandwidth is weak. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show strong performance in the top 25%.

The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff, as reflected in the weak database operations, low Redis SET throughput, and poor static web serving performance. While the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio qualifies this instance for memory-intensive tasks, the shared CPU limits its suitability for sustained high-throughput workloads. This server is best suited for memory-optimized development environments, lightweight caching layers, or offline data processing tasks that require a larger memory footprint relative to compute power but do not demand continuous, dedicated CPU performance.
description: The Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is an x86_64 server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs at 2.0 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. This high 8.0 GB per core ratio is paired with a bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD. Performance benchmarks show a strong single-core stress-ng result in the top 10% and strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, the shared CPU allocation leads to weaker multi-core, database, and Redis performance, alongside poor static web serving throughput. This qualitative resource profile offers a cost-effective option for memory-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources. It is best suited for lightweight caching, memory-intensive development environments, and offline data processing.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is a memory-optimized server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 100 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance alongside a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd features 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 100 GB NVMe SSD for memory-heavy, budget-conscious workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD Genoa processors and local NVMe storage for resource-balanced, budget-conscious workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory with 8.0 GB per core
Bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight caching and memory-heavy development
